learning_outcomes.description: Identify and plan professional learning needs (APST 6.1)
Engage in professional learning and improve practice (APST 6.2)
Seek and apply feedback to improve practice (APST 6.3)
Apply professional learning and improve student learning (APST 6.4)
Engage with external professionals and communities (APST 7.4)

assessments.assessment_title: AT1: Reading and Interpreting Qualitative Research
assessments.task_description: The purpose of this task is to apply research methods principles to the reading and interpretation of a qualitative research article. Students will read a qualitative paper in the field of Health, Sport, and Physical Education, annotate key aspects of the article, and explain how its findings may inform their future practice as teachers and health, sport, and PE professionals.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This is an in-class and written assignment. No computers will be used.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.assessment_title: AT2: Reading and Interpreting Quantitative Research
assessments.task_description: The purpose of this task is to apply research methods principles to the reading and interpretation of a quantitative research article. Students will read a quantitative paper in the field of Health, Sport, and Physical Education, annotate key aspects of the article, and explain how its findings may inform their future practice as teachers and health, sport, and PE professionals.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This is an in-class assignment. No computers are allowed.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.assessment_title: AT3: Research Proposal Poster and Presentation
assessments.task_description: This assessment comprises two components: (A) a Research Proposal Poster and (B) a Research Proposal Presentation. The poster will outline a research proposal that students may wish to undertake. The presentation will consist of a five-minute talk about the proposal, followed by questions.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Specific submission instructions will be made available via Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 1 Lecture: What is Quality Research in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education?

Overarching Themes:

  • What is research?
  • How does research serve as a form of professional learning?
  • What makes something 'quality' research?
  • What are the different types of research?
  • How can I find quality research?
  • What are the roles of professional organisations in research?

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L06
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 1 Lecture: What is Quality Research in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education?
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 1 Lecture: What is Quality Research in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education? Overarching Themes: What is research? How does research serve as a form of professional learning? What makes something 'quality' research? What are the different types of research? How can I find quality research? What are the roles of professional organisations in research?

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 1 Tutorial: Finding Quality Research in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education

Overarching Themes:

  • Identify a topic of interest in health, sport pedagogy and education that is relevant to researchers and teaching professionals
  • Find research that is relevant and credible for that topic
  • Identify key journals and publication outlets in health, sport pedagogy and education
  • Recognise different types of research sources, such as journal articles, books, reports and professional resources
  • Make basic judgements about whether a research source is credible, relevant and useful for understanding or improving practice
  • Investigate professional organisations and professional communities of practice that address quality practice in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L06
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 1 Tutorial: Finding Quality Research in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 1 Tutorial: Finding Quality Research in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education Overarching Themes: Identify a topic of interest in health, sport pedagogy and education that is relevant to researchers and teaching professionals Find research that is relevant and credible for that topic Identify key journals and publication outlets in health, sport pedagogy and education Recognise different types of research sources, such as journal articles, books, reports and professional resources Make basic judgements about whether a research source is credible, relevant and useful for understanding or improving practice Investigate professional organisations and professional communities of practice that address quality practice in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 2 Lecture: How Research Questions Are Framed and Planned

Overarching Themes:

  • Explain how research is used to answer different kinds of questions
  • Understand how a researcher's positionality influences the choices they make about research
  • Identify and understand how different frameworks (e.g. positivist, constructivist, Aboriginal and Indigenous paradigms) shape how researchers frame, understand, and study a topic
  • Identify common research genres (e.g. conversational inquiry, descriptive research, field-based inquiry) and specific research designs (e.g. interview studies, survey studies, practitioner enquiry) that are used to create an overarching plan for research
  • Explain why different frameworks and research designs are suited to answering different research questions

Learning outcomes: L03
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 2 Lecture: How Research Questions Are Framed and Planned
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 2 Lecture: How Research Questions Are Framed and Planned Overarching Themes: Explain how research is used to answer different kinds of questions Understand how a researcher's positionality influences the choices they make about research Identify and understand how different frameworks (e.g. positivist, constructivist, Aboriginal and Indigenous paradigms) shape how researchers frame, understand, and study a topic Identify common research genres (e.g. conversational inquiry, descriptive research, field-based inquiry) and specific research designs (e.g. interview studies, survey studies, practitioner enquiry) that are used to create an overarching plan for research Explain why different frameworks and research designs are suited to answering different research questions

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 2 Tutorial: Understanding the Choices Behind Research

Overarching Themes:

  • Identify a topic of interest in health, sport pedagogy and education that is relevant to researchers and teaching professionals
  • Find research that is relevant and credible for that topic
  • Describe the question, framework and design used in that research output
  • Consider why a researcher or teaching professional may choose a particular framework or design for their inquiry
  • Discuss how researcher positionality and choices shape what a study can show, explain or claim, and how this may inform teaching practice

Learning outcomes: L03
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 2 Tutorial: Understanding the Choices Behind Research
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 2 Tutorial: Understanding the Choices Behind Research Overarching Themes: Identify a topic of interest in health, sport pedagogy and education that is relevant to researchers and teaching professionals Find research that is relevant and credible for that topic Describe the question, framework and design used in that research output Consider why a researcher or teaching professional may choose a particular framework or design for their inquiry Discuss how researcher positionality and choices shape what a study can show, explain or claim, and how this may inform teaching practice

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 3 Lecture: Qualitative Conversational Inquiry - Using Conversations to Understand Teaching, Learning and Experiences

Overarching Themes:

  • Explain why researchers and teaching professionals use conversational and interview-based inquiry in health, sport pedagogy and education
  • Identify the kinds of questions conversational inquiry can help researchers and teaching professionals explore
  • Understand how conversational inquiry may look different when shaped by different frameworks, such as positivist, constructivist and Aboriginal/Indigenous paradigms
  • Identify different types of interviews and conversations, such as structured, semi-structured, unstructured and yarning-based conversations
  • Explain how conversational inquiry can help researchers and teaching professionals understand experience, meaning, relationships and practice

Learning outcomes: L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 3 Lecture: Qualitative Conversational Inquiry - Using Conversations to Understand Teaching, Learning and Experiences
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 3 Lecture: Qualitative Conversational Inquiry - Using Conversations to Understand Teaching, Learning and Experiences Overarching Themes: Explain why researchers and teaching professionals use conversational and interview-based inquiry in health, sport pedagogy and education Identify the kinds of questions conversational inquiry can help researchers and teaching professionals explore Understand how conversational inquiry may look different when shaped by different frameworks, such as positivist, constructivist and Aboriginal/Indigenous paradigms Identify different types of interviews and conversations, such as structured, semi-structured, unstructured and yarning-based conversations Explain how conversational inquiry can help researchers and teaching professionals understand experience, meaning, relationships and practice

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 3 Tutorial: Conversational Inquiry in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education Practice

Overarching Themes:

  • Explore how conversational inquiry has been used in research and teaching practice across health, sport pedagogy, and education
  • Identify the different purposes, frameworks, and interview approaches used in conversational inquiry
  • Consider how different interview and conversational approaches shape what people are invited to share, how they share it, and what might remain unspoken
  • Practise developing open-ended conversational prompts related to a topic of interest in teaching, learning, health, sport pedagogy, or education, and use peer feedback to refine these prompts
  • Reflect on their own positionality, as well as the ethical and relational responsibilities involved in conducting conversational inquiry
  • Practise seeking, providing, and applying constructive feedback as part of developing a more thoughtful, ethical, and responsive conversational inquiry approach

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 3 Tutorial: Conversational Inquiry in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education Practice
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 3 Tutorial: Conversational Inquiry in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education Practice Overarching Themes: Explore how conversational inquiry has been used in research and teaching practice across health, sport pedagogy, and education Identify the different purposes, frameworks, and interview approaches used in conversational inquiry Consider how different interview and conversational approaches shape what people are invited to share, how they share it, and what might remain unspoken Practise developing open-ended conversational prompts related to a topic of interest in teaching, learning, health, sport pedagogy, or education, and use peer feedback to refine these prompts Reflect on their own positionality, as well as the ethical and relational responsibilities involved in conducting conversational inquiry Practise seeking, providing, and applying constructive feedback as part of developing a more thoughtful, ethical, and responsive conversational inquiry approach

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 4 Lecture: Qualitative Unobtrusive Inquiry - Learning from Documents, Artefacts and Existing Materials

Overarching Themes:

  • Explain why researchers and teaching professionals use unobtrusive inquiry in health, sport pedagogy and education
  • Understand how unobtrusive inquiry can be used to study documents, policies, curricula, media, online content, archives and other existing materials
  • Identify common forms of unobtrusive inquiry, such as literature reviews, scoping reviews, content analysis, critical discourse analysis and archival research
  • Recognise how different frameworks shape what researchers notice, analyse and interpret in documents, artefacts and existing materials
  • Explain how unobtrusive inquiry can support research, professional learning, curriculum development and teaching practice

Learning outcomes: L03
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 4 Lecture: Qualitative Unobtrusive Inquiry - Learning from Documents, Artefacts and Existing Materials
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 4 Lecture: Qualitative Unobtrusive Inquiry - Learning from Documents, Artefacts and Existing Materials Overarching Themes: Explain why researchers and teaching professionals use unobtrusive inquiry in health, sport pedagogy and education Understand how unobtrusive inquiry can be used to study documents, policies, curricula, media, online content, archives and other existing materials Identify common forms of unobtrusive inquiry, such as literature reviews, scoping reviews, content analysis, critical discourse analysis and archival research Recognise how different frameworks shape what researchers notice, analyse and interpret in documents, artefacts and existing materials Explain how unobtrusive inquiry can support research, professional learning, curriculum development and teaching practice

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 4 Tutorial: Reviewing and Analysing Texts in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education

Overarching Themes:

  • Explain how reviews can help researchers and teaching professionals understand what is already known about a topic
  • Identify different types of reviews, such as literature reviews and scoping reviews, and describe what they are used for
  • Explore how discourse analysis can be used to examine the language, ideas and assumptions in policies, curricula, media or professional texts
  • Practise making basic observations about patterns, gaps, silences or assumptions in a set of texts, and use peer feedback to refine these observations
  • Discuss how reviews and discourse analysis can inform research, professional learning and teaching practice
  • Practise seeking, providing and applying constructive feedback on their interpretations of texts and how they use analysis to inform research, professional learning and teaching practice

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 4 Tutorial: Reviewing and Analysing Texts in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 4 Tutorial: Reviewing and Analysing Texts in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education Overarching Themes: Explain how reviews can help researchers and teaching professionals understand what is already known about a topic Identify different types of reviews, such as literature reviews and scoping reviews, and describe what they are used for Explore how discourse analysis can be used to examine the language, ideas and assumptions in policies, curricula, media or professional texts Practise making basic observations about patterns, gaps, silences or assumptions in a set of texts, and use peer feedback to refine these observations Discuss how reviews and discourse analysis can inform research, professional learning and teaching practice Practise seeking, providing and applying constructive feedback on their interpretations of texts and how they use analysis to inform research, professional learning and teaching practice

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 5 Lecture: Qualitative Field Inquiry - Studying Practice in Real-World Settings

Overarching Themes:

  • Explain why researchers and teaching professionals study health, sport pedagogy and education in real-world settings
  • Understand how field inquiry can help researchers examine people, practices, relationships, cultures and contexts
  • Identify common forms of field inquiry, such as ethnography, case studies, observations and field-based inquiry
  • Recognise how different frameworks (e.g. positivist, constructivist, Aboriginal/ Indigenous paradigms) shape what researchers notice, record and interpret in field-based research
  • Explain how field inquiry can produce knowledge that is useful for research, professional learning and teaching practice

Learning outcomes: L03
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 5 Lecture: Qualitative Field Inquiry - Studying Practice in Real-World Settings
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 5 Lecture: Qualitative Field Inquiry - Studying Practice in Real-World Settings Overarching Themes: Explain why researchers and teaching professionals study health, sport pedagogy and education in real-world settings Understand how field inquiry can help researchers examine people, practices, relationships, cultures and contexts Identify common forms of field inquiry, such as ethnography, case studies, observations and field-based inquiry Recognise how different frameworks (e.g. positivist, constructivist, Aboriginal/ Indigenous paradigms) shape what researchers notice, record and interpret in field-based research Explain how field inquiry can produce knowledge that is useful for research, professional learning and teaching practice

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 5 Tutorial: Using Practitioner Enquiry to Improve Health, Sport Pedagogy and Educational Practice

Overarching Theme:

  • Explain how practitioner enquiry can help teaching professionals investigate and improve their own practice
  • Identify a practical issue, question or area of curiosity from health, sport pedagogy and education practice
  • Consider what kinds of evidence teaching and pedagogy professionals might collect from their own practice, such as observations, reflections, student work, lesson materials or conversations
  • Discuss how practitioner enquiry can support professional learning, curriculum development and pedagogical change
  • Reflect on the ethical responsibilities involved when teaching professionals research their own practice
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 5 Tutorial: Using Practitioner Enquiry to Improve Health, Sport Pedagogy and Educational Practice
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 5 Tutorial: Using Practitioner Enquiry to Improve Health, Sport Pedagogy and Educational Practice Overarching Theme: Explain how practitioner enquiry can help teaching professionals investigate and improve their own practice Identify a practical issue, question or area of curiosity from health, sport pedagogy and education practice Consider what kinds of evidence teaching and pedagogy professionals might collect from their own practice, such as observations, reflections, student work, lesson materials or conversations Discuss how practitioner enquiry can support professional learning, curriculum development and pedagogical change Reflect on the ethical responsibilities involved when teaching professionals research their own practice

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 6 Lecture: AT1 - Reading and Interpreting Qualitative Research

In-Class Assessment.

Learning outcomes: L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 6 Lecture: AT1 - Reading and Interpreting Qualitative Research
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 6 Lecture: AT1 - Reading and Interpreting Qualitative Research In-Class Assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 6 Tutorial: Quantitative Descriptive and Correlational Research - Describing Patterns and Relationships in Research

Overarching Themes:

  • Explain how descriptive research can help researchers and teaching professionals understand what is happening in health, sport pedagogy and education
  • Explain how correlational research can help researchers and teaching professionals examine relationships between different factors
  • Identify the kinds of questions descriptive and correlational research can help answer
  • Interpret basic descriptive and correlational findings in descriptive research published by a professional organisation in the field
  • Discuss how descriptive and correlational research can inform professional learning, decision-making and teaching practice

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L06
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 6 Tutorial: Quantitative Descriptive and Correlational Research - Describing Patterns and Relationships in Research
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 6 Tutorial: Quantitative Descriptive and Correlational Research - Describing Patterns and Relationships in Research Overarching Themes: Explain how descriptive research can help researchers and teaching professionals understand what is happening in health, sport pedagogy and education Explain how correlational research can help researchers and teaching professionals examine relationships between different factors Identify the kinds of questions descriptive and correlational research can help answer Interpret basic descriptive and correlational findings in descriptive research published by a professional organisation in the field Discuss how descriptive and correlational research can inform professional learning, decision-making and teaching practice

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 7 Lecture: Quantitative Quasi-Experimental Research - Studying Change in Real-World Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education Settings

Overarching Themes:

  • Explain why researchers and teaching professionals use quasi-experimental research in health, sport pedagogy and education
  • Understand how quasi-experimental research can be used to study change, impact or improvement in real-world settings
  • Identify common features of quasi-experimental research, such as comparison groups, pre- and post-testing, interventions and naturally occurring groups
  • Recognise how quasi-experimental research differs from fully experimental research
  • Discuss what quasi-experimental research can and cannot claim about whether something “works”

Learning outcomes: L03
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 7 Lecture: Quantitative Quasi-Experimental Research - Studying Change in Real-World Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education Settings
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 7 Lecture: Quantitative Quasi-Experimental Research - Studying Change in Real-World Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education Settings Overarching Themes: Explain why researchers and teaching professionals use quasi-experimental research in health, sport pedagogy and education Understand how quasi-experimental research can be used to study change, impact or improvement in real-world settings Identify common features of quasi-experimental research, such as comparison groups, pre- and post-testing, interventions and naturally occurring groups Recognise how quasi-experimental research differs from fully experimental research Discuss what quasi-experimental research can and cannot claim about whether something “works”

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 7 Tutorial: Understanding Whether Something Works in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education Practice

Overarching Themes:

  • Identify the purpose, design and key features of a quasi-experimental study
  • Explain what change or impact a quasi-experimental study is trying to examine
  • Interpret basic findings from a quasi-experimental study in health, sport pedagogy and education
  • Discuss the strengths and limitations of using quasi-experimental research in real-world teaching and learning settings
  • Consider how quasi-experimental research can inform professional decision-making, programme improvement and teaching practice

Learning outcomes: L01, L03
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 7 Tutorial: Understanding Whether Something Works in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education Practice
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 7 Tutorial: Understanding Whether Something Works in Health, Sport Pedagogy and Education Practice Overarching Themes: Identify the purpose, design and key features of a quasi-experimental study Explain what change or impact a quasi-experimental study is trying to examine Interpret basic findings from a quasi-experimental study in health, sport pedagogy and education Discuss the strengths and limitations of using quasi-experimental research in real-world teaching and learning settings Consider how quasi-experimental research can inform professional decision-making, programme improvement and teaching practice

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 8 Lecture: AT2 - Reading and Interpreting Quantitative Research

In-class assessment task.

Learning outcomes: L03, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 8 Lecture: AT2 - Reading and Interpreting Quantitative Research
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 8 Lecture: AT2 - Reading and Interpreting Quantitative Research In-class assessment task.

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 8 Tutorial: Synthesising Research and Developing a Study Purpose

Overarching Theme:

  • Organise selected research articles around a topic of interest in health, sport pedagogy and education
  • Compare findings across different studies to identify patterns, extensions, tensions and possibilities
  • Begin critically synthesising the selected literature into a clear account of what is known and what could be explored
  • Develop a purpose statement for a potential study based on the selected literature
  • Draft specific research question(s) that connect to the literature, purpose and possible research design

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 8 Tutorial: Synthesising Research and Developing a Study Purpose
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 8 Tutorial: Synthesising Research and Developing a Study Purpose Overarching Theme: Organise selected research articles around a topic of interest in health, sport pedagogy and education Compare findings across different studies to identify patterns, extensions, tensions and possibilities Begin critically synthesising the selected literature into a clear account of what is known and what could be explored Develop a purpose statement for a potential study based on the selected literature Draft specific research question(s) that connect to the literature, purpose and possible research design

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 9 Lecture: Choosing a Research Design Responsibly

Overarching Themes:

  • Explain how researchers and teaching professionals choose a research design based on a study purpose and research question(s)
  • Revisit common research designs, such as conversational inquiry, field inquiry, unobtrusive inquiry, descriptive research, correlational research and quasi-experimental research
  • Explain why some research designs are better suited to particular purposes and research question(s) than others
  • Understand the ethical responsibilities involved in planning research with people, practices, documents, data or communities
  • Recognise the role of self-reflection and reflexivity in making thoughtful and ethical research decisions

Learning outcomes: L03
learning_activities.topic_title: Wk 9 Lecture: Choosing a Research Design Responsibly
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 9 Lecture: Choosing a Research Design Responsibly Overarching Themes: Explain how researchers and teaching professionals choose a research design based on a study purpose and research question(s) Revisit common research designs, such as conversational inquiry, field inquiry, unobtrusive inquiry, descriptive research, correlational research and quasi-experimental research Explain why some research designs are better suited to particular purposes and research question(s) than others Understand the ethical responsibilities involved in planning research with people, practices, documents, data or communities Recognise the role of self-reflection and reflexivity in making thoughtful and ethical research decisions

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 9 Tutorial: Designing an Ethical Study

Overarching Themes:

  • Review the purpose statement and research question(s) developed from their selected literature
  • Consider which research design is most appropriate for their study purpose and research question(s)
  • Explain why their chosen design is suitable for what they want to understand, explore, describe, examine or evaluate
  • Identify key ethical issues that may need to be considered in their proposed study, including how they might consult with relevant community members, participants or professional stakeholders in respectful and meaningful ways
  • Reflect on how their own assumptions, experiences, values and professional position may shape the way they design the study
  • Consider how consultation with community members, participants or professional stakeholders can strengthen the ethical, relational and practical relevance of their proposed study

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 9 Tutorial: Designing an Ethical Study Overarching Themes: Review the purpose statement and research question(s) developed from their selected literature Consider which research design is most appropriate for their study purpose and research question(s) Explain why their chosen design is suitable for what they want to understand, explore, describe, examine or evaluate Identify key ethical issues that may need to be considered in their proposed study, including how they might consult with relevant community members, participants or professional stakeholders in respectful and meaningful ways Reflect on how their own assumptions, experiences, values and professional position may shape the way they design the study Consider how consultation with community members, participants or professional stakeholders can strengthen the ethical, relational and practical relevance of their proposed study

learning_activities.topic:
Wk 10 Lec/Tut: Pitching Your Research Proposal

Overarching Themes:

  • Present a draft research proposal that identifies a research purpose, research question(s), framework, design, ethical considerations, positionality and potential implications for professional practice
  • Explain how their proposed study is justified by the literature they have reviewed
  • Explain why their chosen framework and research design are suited to the purpose and research question(s) of the proposed study
  • Reflect on how their own position, experiences, values and professional context shape the study and its ethical responsibilities
  • Discuss how the proposed study may inform, challenge or improve professional teaching practice
  • Practise giving, receiving and applying constructive feedback on the clarity, coherence, justification, ethics and professional significance of proposed studies

Learning outcomes: L02, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_description: Wk 10 Lec/Tut: Pitching Your Research Proposal Overarching Themes: Present a draft research proposal that identifies a research purpose, research question(s), framework, design, ethical considerations, positionality and potential implications for professional practice Explain how their proposed study is justified by the literature they have reviewed Explain why their chosen framework and research design are suited to the purpose and research question(s) of the proposed study Reflect on how their own position, experiences, values and professional context shape the study and its ethical responsibilities Discuss how the proposed study may inform, challenge or improve professional teaching practice Practise giving, receiving and applying constructive feedback on the clarity, coherence, justification, ethics and professional significance of proposed studies
